summary: The test suite is impressively comprehensive, covering all seven functions called for by the requirements (ConvertTo-RegexFromGlob, Test-GlobMatch, Get-LabelsForFile, Get-PrLabels, New-LabelRule, Import-LabelConfig, and an end-to-end integration suite). All major requirements are exercised: glob patterns (*, **, ?), multiple labels per file, priority ordering, deduplication, configurable JSON rules, and mock PR scenarios. Rigor is strong — path-separator normalization, dot-escaping, empty input, negative priority, missing config files, and catch-all rules are all tested. Design is clean: Describe blocks map 1:1 to exported functions, BeforeAll/AfterAll handle fixtures properly with $script: scoping, temp files are cleaned up, and test names are descriptive. Minor gaps that prevent a perfect rigor score: no test for malformed/invalid JSON in Import-LabelConfig, priority tie-breaking is unspecified, an empty rules array passed to Get-PrLabels is not covered, and there is no test verifying that Get-LabelsForFile returns an actual empty array (typed) rather than $null when nothing matches. Overall this is a trustworthy suite that would catch most regressions.
